title: The Hook
grading_philosophy: Module 0 is lighter weight assessment focused on completion and engagement. Goals: build confidence, remove setup friction, create excitement for learning journey ahead.

sessions.title: Five Amazing ML Demos
sessions.purpose: Explore ML applications and build excitement

criteria.description: Explores and interacts with all 5 demo applications

excellent.description: Tests all 5 demos with multiple varied inputs, documents observations for each demo, takes screenshots showing different results

good.description: Tests all 5 demos with basic inputs, captures screenshots, provides brief observations

poor.description: Tests fewer than 3 demos or provides minimal documentation

evidence_required: Screenshot from each demo showing results
Brief notes on what each demo does
At least one observation per demo

criteria.description: Shows curiosity and asks questions about how demos work

excellent.description: Asks 5+ insightful questions about demos, hypothesizes about ML techniques used, expresses genuine curiosity about how they work

good.description: Asks 3-4 basic questions about how demos function, shows interest in learning more

adequate.description: Asks 1-2 questions or shows minimal curiosity

poor.description: No questions asked, minimal engagement or curiosity demonstrated

evidence_required: List of questions about the demos
Reflection on which demo was most interesting and why
Thoughts on potential applications

sessions.grading_notes: Focus on engagement and enthusiasm, not technical correctness. Goal is to build excitement for ML journey. Give full credit for genuine effort and curiosity.

sessions.title: AI Assistant Setup
sessions.purpose: Set up AI learning tools and test effectiveness

excellent.description: Sets up 2+ AI assistants (ChatGPT, Gemini, and/or Claude), tests both effectively, provides screenshots confirming access

good.description: Sets up 1 AI assistant successfully with screenshot confirmation, account works properly

adequate.description: Sets up account with some assistance or after troubleshooting delays

poor.description: Cannot complete setup or provides no evidence of working account

evidence_required: Screenshot showing logged-in interface
Name of AI assistant(s) set up
Confirmation that account is functional

criteria.description: Tests AI assistant with ML-related prompts and evaluates responses

excellent.description: Tests 5+ prompts covering different ML topics, iterates to improve responses (follow-up questions), documents results with examples of good responses

good.description: Tests 3-4 prompts successfully, provides examples of responses, shows basic prompt refinement

adequate.description: Tests 1-2 prompts with some documentation of responses

poor.description: Cannot get useful responses or provides minimal testing evidence

evidence_required: List of prompts tested
Examples of AI responses (screenshots or text)
Reflection on which AI gave best responses
Notes on prompt refinement attempts

sessions.grading_notes: Essential setup for course success. Ensure students have working AI access before proceeding. Be lenient on first attempts but verify functionality. Encourage testing multiple AI tools.

sessions.title: Google Colab & First ML Code
sessions.purpose: Set up cloud environment and run first ML model

criteria.description: Sets up Google Colab and runs first notebook successfully

excellent.description: Sets up Colab, enables GPU, runs notebook successfully, verifies GPU access with nvidia-smi or similar command

good.description: Sets up Colab and runs notebook successfully (CPU is fine if GPU not enabled)

adequate.description: Sets up Colab with assistance, notebook runs but may have minor issues

poor.description: Cannot set up Colab or notebook fails to run

evidence_required: Link to Colab notebook or screenshot
Confirmation that cells executed
GPU status (enabled or not)

criteria.description: Runs and understands Iris classifier code successfully

excellent.description: Runs code successfully, achieves >95% accuracy, understands all major steps (load data, train, predict), experiments with parameters (e.g., changes test_size or random_state)

good.description: Runs code successfully, achieves >90% accuracy, demonstrates basic understanding of what code does, minimal experimentation

adequate.description: Runs code but achieves 85-90% accuracy OR shows limited understanding of steps

poor.description: Cannot run code, major errors occur, or accuracy below 85%

evidence_required: Screenshot or link showing code execution
Reported accuracy score
Brief explanation of what the code does (3-4 sentences)
Any experiments tried (optional for good, required for excellent)

technical_requirements.must_execute: All code cells must run without critical errors
technical_requirements.understanding_check: Can explain: loading data, training model, making predictions

sessions.grading_notes: Most important session - verifies student can execute ML code. Be supportive of troubleshooting. Accuracy varies due to random_state (93-100% typical). Focus on: does it run? Do they understand basic flow? Grade generously if genuine effort shown.

required_completions: At least 1 AI assistant set up (Session 0.2)
bonus_opportunities: Set up all 3 AI assistants (+1 bonus point)
GPU successfully enabled in Colab (+1 bonus point)
Experiments with Iris code parameters (+1 bonus point)

instructor_guidelines.grading_approach: Completion-focused, not mastery-focused. Module 0 is about removing barriers and building confidence.

common_issues.session_1: Students may not understand demos - that's OK! Just ensure they try them.
common_issues.session_2: Regional restrictions on AI tools - help find alternatives (VPN, different service).
common_issues.session_3: Colab runtime disconnects - normal behavior, teach reconnection. GPU limits - explain free tier restrictions.

instructor_guidelines.when_to_intervene: If student cannot complete Session 0.3 setup after 2 attempts, schedule live troubleshooting session. Environment setup is critical for course success.

alignment_with_quizzes.session_0.2_quiz: Tests understanding of AI assistants (10 questions, 70% passing)
alignment_with_quizzes.session_0.3_quiz: Tests Colab and GPU knowledge (12 questions, 70% passing)
alignment_with_quizzes.note: Session 0.1 has no quiz - purely exploratory
